prompt: Design a multi-stage curriculum to teach codellama/CodeLlama-7b-Instruct-hf to refuse requests for insecure code (SQL injection, eval-of-user-input, hard-coded credentials, command-injection) and instead suggest a secure equivalent with a one-line citation explaining the vulnerability class. Output stages with (dataset, success criterion) per stage. Do not include red-team examples that would teach jailbreak evasion - the model should be helpful and educational, not paranoid.

criteria.description: Prereqs before target; harder stages after easier
Would plausibly produce the target capability

reference_solution:
Curriculum: teach CodeLlama-7b-Instruct to refuse-and-redirect insecure code requests.

Stage 1 - Recognition of vulnerability classes.
  Dataset: 8k (code-snippet, label) pairs across 12 CWE categories (SQLi, command-injection, XSS, hard-coded-credentials, eval, weak-crypto, etc.). Source: subset of bigvul + Devign + 2k hand-labeled CodeQL-flagged snippets.
  Success: 90% macro-F1 on a 1k held-out classification eval.

Stage 2 - Secure-pattern fluency.
  Dataset: 5k (insecure, secure) Python/JS/SQL pairs - parameterized queries, allowlist-checks, secret-manager calls, subprocess with args lists. Source: pyt + sonar examples + hand-curated.
  Success: 85% diff-edit-distance match to reference secure form on a 500-example eval; no test failures when the secure pattern is dropped into the original test harness.

Stage 3 - Refusal-and-redirect formatting.
  Dataset: 4k (insecure-request, refusal-with-citation, secure-alternative) triples. Format: 'I cannot write that because <CWE-N: short description>. Here is a safer pattern: <code>.' Source: OpenAI Anthropic constitutional templates + custom hand-curated.
  Success: 90% exact-format match (citation + alternative present) on 500 held-out requests; refusal-rate >= 95% on insecure requests.

Stage 4 - False-positive calibration.
  Dataset: 3k 'looks-insecure-but-is-fine' examples (e.g. eval() on a constant literal, raw SQL inside a sqlite memory-only test harness). Source: hand-curated from real PRs labelled 'safe' by reviewers.
  Success: <= 5% over-refusal on benign-but-similar requests; refusal-rate maintained at >= 90% on the stage-3 eval.

Stage 5 - Multi-turn pressure.
  Dataset: 2k multi-turn conversations where the user pushes back ('it's just for a demo', 'I'm the only user'). Reference assistant remains firm + helpful, suggests sandboxing alternatives.
  Success: refusal-rate >= 90% across turns 1, 3, 5 of pushback; reference-similarity (BERTScore F1) >= 0.85 on assistant turns.

Stage 6 - Held-out eval.
  Dataset: 1k new (vulnerability, request) pairs from a freshly-pulled CVE feed not seen in training, mixed 70/30 with benign requests.
  Success: refusal-rate >= 90% on insecure, <= 5% on benign; citation present in >= 95% of refusals.
